text: Gene editing is rewriting the future of sickle cell therapy. CRISPR, a powerful gene-editing tool, is being harnessed in a controlled way to treat sickle cell disease. But how does it work? Imagine editing genes outside the body before reintroducing them. In ex vivo therapies, blood cells are modified in labs, then returned to patients under strict medical oversight. But let's be clear—these treatments are not one-size-fits-all and still require extensive research. Though early results are promising, the journey towards a cure is filled with caution and care. Curious about the future of gene editing? Hit like and follow for more insights!
